implemented_milestones: v1.0.26 Streamlit finalization and lifecycle safety
v1.2.2 runtime and release evidence closure
v1.3.1 release-state convergence and repository hygiene

next_milestone.name: PDF.js Reader runtime verification and future read-only hardening

pr_2.evidence: PR #2 merged the v1.3.0 Reader/PDF vertical slice into main on 2026-07-18.

local_smoke.evidence: v1.4.0 final smoke validation: 98 passed, 0 warnings, 0 failed on 2026-07-21.

local_pytest.evidence: v1.4.0 final full pytest validation: 526 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped in 34.57 seconds on 2026-07-21.

frontend_dependency_setup.evidence: npm ci added 495 packages and audited 496 from the committed lockfile with Node 24.18.0 and npm 11.16.0 on 2026-07-21; 12 audit findings were reported.

frontend_lint.evidence: npm run lint exited 0 with no findings after the clean npm ci install on 2026-07-21.

frontend_test_build.evidence: Vinext production build passed, emitted the local PDF.js worker asset, and 25 Node tests passed with 0 failed, 0 skipped, and 0 cancelled on 2026-07-21.

repository_hygiene.evidence: The standalone tracked-entry check passed for 219 present tracked entries; smoke repeated the same tracked-only check without reading user-data contents.

tracker_export.evidence: The standard-library exporter produced a deterministic 25-row UTF-8 CSV with the required header and no forbidden private values.

github_actions.evidence: The PR #2 workflow completed successfully on 2026-07-18; Python and frontend jobs both concluded success.

post_merge_main_github_actions.evidence: The main push workflow for merge commit 9663c8cd completed successfully on 2026-07-18; Python and frontend jobs both concluded success.

tasks.evidence: Streamlit save, draft, lifecycle, and regression contracts are documented and tested.
tasks.disposition: Preserve as the Streamlit regression floor.

tasks.evidence: Lifecycle and recovery contracts retain confirmation gates and non-destructive defaults.
tasks.disposition: Preserve containment and byte-preserving recovery behavior.

tasks.evidence: Setup, Node resolution, local launch, and diagnostic scripts have focused contract tests.
tasks.disposition: Keep clean-PC rehearsal as an independent gate.

tasks.evidence: Scanner and hash-reuse tests preserve local deterministic scanning and explicit enrichment.
tasks.disposition: Retain scanner and cache regressions.

tasks.evidence: Reader state-machine and note-state tests cover explicit save and draft preservation.
tasks.disposition: Use this contract as the parity floor for future writes.

tasks.evidence: Deterministic Tag Book aliases and evidence-bearing candidates exist; measured governance quality does not.
tasks.disposition: Complete auditable governance with a representative fixture corpus during retrieval work.

tasks.evidence: Read-only health and library endpoints have strict schemas and failure-state tests.
tasks.disposition: Keep mutation behind a future command boundary.

tasks.evidence: Collection and detail endpoints have pagination, archive, ordering, and error contract tests.
tasks.disposition: Preserve existing route and response compatibility.

tasks.evidence: Rich local metadata schemas and read models are covered without read-time enrichment.
tasks.disposition: Measure completeness before widening schemas.

tasks.evidence: The desktop shell, typed client, shared components, and read-only bridge are implemented and tested.
tasks.disposition: Treat the shell as migration infrastructure, not write parity.

tasks.evidence: The full local gate and hosted workflow include Python and frontend validation paths.
tasks.disposition: Keep partial modes explicitly non-release-qualified.

tasks.evidence: Loopback launch, workflow dispatch, local gate, runtime, and PR #1 evidence are recorded.
tasks.disposition: Preserve as historical v1.2.2 evidence.

tasks.evidence: Managed PDF delivery, byte ranges, Reader navigation, failure states, and no-write scope are tested.
tasks.disposition: Preserve the managed-root boundary and native fallback.

tasks.evidence: Current documents and conditional tests distinguish PR CI, main CI, restore, tag, and publication states.
tasks.disposition: v1.3.1 release-state convergence replaces the stale provisional snapshot.

tasks.evidence: The accidental root artifact is removed and tracked-file hygiene runs through smoke validation.
tasks.disposition: Maintain narrow tracked-entry rules and focused tests.

tasks.evidence: Canonical JSON now exports deterministic standard-library CSV with controlled fields.
tasks.disposition: Use the CSV as the mechanical external-tracker handoff.

tasks.evidence: A restore checklist and automated readiness checks exist, but no genuine clean-PC rehearsal is recorded.
tasks.disposition: User performs the complete rehearsal on a genuinely clean machine.

tasks.evidence: The PDF.js Reader exposes development-only request-count, Range-response, load, render, and cancellation diagnostics; real-browser request inspection and index-read latency remain unmeasured.
tasks.disposition: Complete browser request inspection with a disposable or approved real PDF; measure index latency only if a later bottleneck requires it.

tasks.evidence: v1.4.0 implements pinned pdfjs-dist with a bundled local worker, bounded controls, cleanup, diagnostics, tests, and a conditional native fallback.
tasks.disposition: Keep advanced PDF.js layers and write features deferred; complete the pending manual runtime checklist separately.

tasks.evidence: The API remains GET-only and no single write-command boundary is implemented.
tasks.disposition: Design one tested command service before exposing web mutations.

tasks.evidence: Development launch paths exist; distributable packaging and clean-machine operation are unproven.
tasks.disposition: Revisit after restore evidence and interface direction stabilize.

tasks.evidence: Cross-context local retrieval and a benchmark are not implemented.
tasks.disposition: Implement and measure retrieval in a separately approved milestone.

tasks.evidence: No external activation, retention, workflow-replacement, or value evidence is recorded.
tasks.disposition: Collect dogfood evidence before a small external researcher cohort.

tasks.evidence: Graph and AI-assisted features remain outside the implemented product scope.
tasks.disposition: Require closed retrieval and product-validation gates first.

tasks.evidence: No approved v1.4.0 tag or published GitHub release exists.
tasks.disposition: Create an immutable checkpoint only after explicit approval and required evidence.
